basics.name: Tapas Pal
basics.label: Cryptographer · Group Leader, Secure Computation & Communication, KASTEL SRL, KIT
basics.summary: Cryptographer working on the foundations and real-world deployment of advanced public-key encryption. I design provably secure functional, attribute-based, and registered encryption schemes that decentralize trust, make computation on encrypted data verifiable, and bring strong privacy guarantees to cloud platforms, digital infrastructure, and AI systems. The work spans rigorous theory (pairing- and lattice-based constructions with formal security proofs) and applied impact (multiple international patents and industry technology due-diligence). Author of 20+ peer-reviewed papers at leading venues including Crypto, Eurocrypt, Asiacrypt, PKC, and the Journal of Cryptology.

work.name: KASTEL Security Research Labs, Karlsruhe Institute of Technology (KIT)
work.summary: Lead a research group on privacy-enhancing technologies, covering research strategy, proposal coordination, and mentoring of early-career researchers.

work.name: Social Informatics Laboratories, NTT Corporation
work.summary: Computing on encrypted data and advanced functional-encryption primitives (host: Dr. Ryo Nishimaki).

work.name: Cyber Security Group, University of St. Gallen
work.summary: Supervised the project on multi-client attribute-based unbounded inner-product functional encryption.

work.name: R. C. Bose Centre for Cryptology & Security, ISI Kolkata
work.summary: Functional encryption and its applications (host: Prof. Mridul Nandi).

work.name: Cryptography & Information Security (CIS) Lab, NTT Research, Inc.

volunteer.summary: Technical due-diligence on a password-less access-control platform (MyCena); startup evaluation and advisory.
Technical due-diligence on cryptographic image-authenticity technology (TrustNXT); startup evaluation and advisory.

education.summary: Advisor: Prof. Ratna Dutta. Thesis: Designing Provably Secure Advanced Cryptographic Primitives — Witness Encryption, Fully Homomorphic Encryption, and Functional Encryption. Defended 6 October 2021.

education.institution: Scottish Church College, University of Calcutta

projects.name: Multi-Client Unbounded Attribute-Based Inner Product Functional Encryption
